title: Anticoagulation documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports documenting clinician-performed benefit-risk discussion, adherence and control review, renal and bleeding context, annual reassessment, and documented decisions without generating a medicine choice or dose.
red_flags.rationale: The NICE section supports clinician-documented anticoagulation benefit-risk discussion, adherence and control review, renal and bleeding context, patient preference, annual reassessment, and recorded decisions in AF only; it does not generalize to every indication or produce a medicine or dose.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting clinician-performed benefit-risk discussion, adherence and control review, renal and bleeding context, annual reassessment, and documented decisions without generating a medicine choice or dose.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1-1.6.17 and 1.11.1-1.11.2 - anticoagulation discussion and review

source_limitations: Indication, medicine, dose, route, frequency, duration, adherence, last dose, renal and liver function, blood count, INR or TTR, bleeding, thrombosis, interactions, pregnancy, procedure, interruption, reversal, prescribing, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
